subject: Accounting
title: Goodwill: The Invisible Asset
description: A fast breakdown of Goodwill: The Invisible Asset for builders.

hook.text: A company buys another for $10 billion, but the hard assets are only worth $6 billion. Where's the rest?

buildup.text: The $4 billion gap is goodwill—paying extra for brand reputation, customer loyalty, and talent.

discovery.text: Goodwill sits on the balance sheet as an intangible asset after an acquisition.

twist.text: If the acquisition flops, goodwill gets 'impaired'—written down, destroying reported equity.

climax.text: Massive goodwill write-downs signal that a deal was overpriced. Markets punish it immediately.

punchline.text: Goodwill is hope on a balance sheet.

quiz.question: What does goodwill represent on a balance sheet?
options: The premium paid above net asset value in an acquisition
